recaps.text: Previously: Zac emerged as the ranker known as Super Brother Man and established Port Atwood with the demon Ogras among his principal allies. He entered the new System-driven world as an independent human power, but the Integration scattered his family and left other peoples and incursions competing for survival on Earth. His immediate priorities are protecting Port Atwood and finding his missing relatives.

Zac enters New Washington under the name Monk, hoping the rebuilding government can help him locate Greenworth and his missing family. Its leaders want Port Atwood's intelligence and cooperation, but both sides remain wary. In Greenworth, Zac finds his father's home looted and learns that returned cultivators murdered Robert while seizing control of the town. Zac kills the ruling council, and a former captive kills the complicit mayor. Joanna Anderson becomes his first contracted recruit, followed by eighty rescued women who form the Atwood Valkyries. Zac reveals his identities to them, returns through New Washington, and takes the recruits to Port Atwood. The government accepts only a conditional relationship while it investigates his actions. At home, Zac learns that a hostile Zhix hive occupies a nearby island, creating an immediate threat as he continues searching for Kenzie.

Zac confronts the neighboring Zhix and gains a fragile nonaggression agreement from their Holiness. Ibtep becomes the hive's liaison, uses Port Atwood's Nexus node to take the Seeker class, and begins learning about the System. The Zhix warn that powerful members of their species may serve surviving Dominators. Port Atwood expands by rescuing endangered settlers, while Sap Trang joins its field operations and Calrin prepares to represent its commercial interests. Ogras emerges from seclusion and accompanies Zac, Ibtep, Calrin, and Sap to New Washington's auction. There, suspicion of the nonhuman delegation leads to conflict, but Zac protects Ibtep without killing the attackers. The party meets Thea Marshall, the leader of Billyville, and Ishiate seeking scattered communities. Port Atwood gains possible links to both a ratman-incursion campaign and the Ishiate settlement of Cogstown as the auction begins.

At the auction, Zac buys a mysterious life-draining material that attracts his spirit axe and identifies a northern teleporter that could carry him around the undead territory toward Kenzie. Government leaders acknowledge that Port Atwood stopped an incursion, but some privately consider using Zac's family to control him. The government proposes a centralized teleporter network with inspections and permanent oversight. Henry Marshall counters with a decentralized network for independent lords, but Zac commits to neither plan. He instead coerces the northern teleporter owner into granting future access. After mercenaries try to steal his Cosmos sack, Zac openly recruits specialists under the Super Brother Man identity. Ogras and Calrin pursue trade, Sap Trang runs the public recruitment effort, and Port Atwood's connection to its powerful lord becomes public. The settlement gains technical workers and resources, but its independence leaves relations with New Washington and the Marshalls unsettled.

Ogras secretly extracts Emma McHale from New Washington after she asks for sanctuary, and Julia follows through the teleporter, becoming confined at Port Atwood. The operation sharply worsens relations with the government. Zac prepares for the final defense wave, recruits Cogstown tinkerers, and deploys the Valkyries and about one hundred Zhix alongside Port Atwood's demons. The expected horde is replaced by a special challenge involving three intelligent incursions: an undead army, a stone-like force, and the Church of Everlasting Dao. The attackers breach the wall, and Zac infiltrates the undead camp while Ogras, Alea, Janos, and Sap Trang assault the Church contingent. Zac forces the corpse lord to retreat but suffers a miasma-corrupted wound. Ogras kills the Church leader, and Alea helps Zac kill the final enemy commander. Port Atwood survives and completes the challenge, but a Church infiltrator's imitation of Janos proves that shapeshifters may already be embedded among human forces.

The victory makes Zac the world's first lord, upgrades Port Atwood into a global city, and opens his Road to Sovereignty. The city receives the Towers of Myriad Dao, a repository of skills and inheritances managed by the volatile treasure spirit Brazla. Zac establishes ministries, placing Ilvir over war and Alea over justice, while the city's contribution-based administration begins to take shape. His miasma wound remains beyond ordinary treatment, so he leaves alone and travels under the name David toward the undead frontier. Purifier Wang cannot cure the injury, and local rulers turn on Zac after he forces access to the clinic. Meanwhile, Ogras screens Port Atwood with Spring Root, uncovers multiple infiltrators, and leads an expedition against Refugees Harbor. Janos detects mass mind control, Ogras kills the hypnotist holding the town's children, and the captives are freed. One resident then identifies herself as Hannah and claims a past relationship with Zac.

Zac crosses the dead zone while his wound absorbs ever more miasma. A System quest forces him to kill ten thousand undead in ten minutes, and the resulting overload nearly destroys him. The corruption condenses into an unexplained bead joined to his pathways. After killing the returning undead lord, Zac becomes emaciated as the new core drains his vitality. Sui secretly recognizes him, sustains him with healing energy, and brings him to a protected monastery. Its wounded abbot explains that the undead incursion still includes several lords and a lich king. Rather than purge the opposing energies, Zac chooses to balance life and death. A seed from the monastery's healing lotus stabilizes the core. The abbot then directs him to a government town holding Greenworth cultivators. Zac finds Kenzie's name on a trial list and confirms that she is alive but imprisoned on an attempted-murder charge. He enters the public hearing in disguise and sees that she has been abused in custody.

Zac interrupts Kenzie's trial in Kingsbury, kills the abusive mayor, destroys the hidden teleporter, and protects his sister from a shapeshifter attack. An unknown blue device shields Kenzie from the explosion and then disappears into her head. Zac rescues her friend Lila, and Olivia joins them before all three relocate to Port Atwood. Kenzie later reveals that a secret assistant called Jeeves has guided her cultivation and improved her class. Ogras identifies their mother's amulet as a technocrat insignia, suggesting an unconfirmed connection between Leandra, Kenzie's assistant, and that hidden faction. Port Atwood has meanwhile grown into a network of settlements with a formal merit exchange. Zac reunites with Hannah but ends their relationship. David, her surviving tutorial companion, feeds her resentment, and she receives a betrayal-centered quest. Zac also discovers an unstable mystic-realm entrance and begins buying costly life-attuned crystals to develop his balanced core.

Zac enters his first sovereignty trial and is summoned to an alien kingdom threatened by an invading army and an ascended beast. He forces a temporary ceasefire rather than slaughtering the soldiers, then enters the beast's sealed city. A powerful purple-haired pugilist challenges him for the right to kill the level-79 Star Ox. Zac wins their duel and kills the beast despite its horn beam and explosive defenses. The pugilist's father appears through an immensely powerful clone, recognizes Zac as a progenitor, and gives him a bracelet that hides his unusual core from most observers. Zac returns to Earth with a tower token and a Dao treasure. After six days of feeding equal life and death energy into the core, it divides into gold and black halves and becomes a recognized specialty core. Before he can determine its purpose, Kenzie reports that Hannah has vanished after signs of distress at her home.

Hannah ambushes Zac with a life-draining dagger as part of a plan to inherit Port Atwood's lordship. Ogras stops David from finishing the attack, and Zac's death-attuned core releases miasma that saves him. David is exposed as a shapeshifter and destroyed without producing a normal System kill. Hannah is imprisoned, but Janos later finds evidence that she endured advanced mental manipulation. The real David is discovered alive and severely tortured beneath his home. Zac's transformed Draugr state and level-one status reveal that his Duplicity Core can support a second class. With Ogras guarding against interference, Zac kills an E-grade beast, gains the Apex Hunter title, and advances enough to choose the defensive Undying Bulkwork class. Its pathways settle into the death-attuned half of his core, creating a second class path alongside Hatchet Man. The wider shapeshifter threat remains active even though no replacement is found inside Port Atwood.

Zac learns that exhausting his miasma returns him from Draugr to human form, though the change leaves him unconscious and is too slow for combat. He distributes responsibilities and resources before the Limited Treasure Hunt, gives Emily a Fruit of Ascension, and receives Zhix gifts from Ibtep intended to reduce conflict with other hives. Lila and several Refugees Harbor cultivators choose to enter despite Zac's warning that he cannot protect them. Zac uses his Dao treasure to advance his Seed of Sharpness. He appoints Kenzie and Ogras as proxies with authority to form alliances, orders Kenzie to remain near Ogras, and requires Ogras to find the remaining shapeshifter before attempting the Umbra inheritance. The month-long hunt transports Zac alone to the ruins of a vast mountain sect. Its rules reward valuable discoveries and dangerous kills, permit withdrawal outside forced arenas, and make the higher structures likely centers of conflict. Zac begins the hunt while Port Atwood's infiltration and political threats remain unresolved.
